si.t: A renderer torture test with loud styling and chart edge cases.
2D/3D bar and column variants, hidden rows, percent stacked, negative values
Date axes, gaps, trendlines, markers, stacked areas, 3D rotations
Pie, 3D pie, doughnut, projected pie/bar splits, exploded slices
Scatter line/marker blends, log axis, bubble sizing, mixed scales
Filled radar, wireframe surface, 3D surface gradients, matrix data
High/low/close stock chart, combo chart, secondary axis
Conditional formats, merged cells, formulas, comments, hidden rows/cols
Some charts include blanks, hidden rows, negatives, and oversized fills on purpose.
LibreOffice render sanity-checks this workbook after generation.
If Excel repairs anything, that is a bug worth investigating.
Hidden rows, percent stacks, negatives, zeros, and too many colors.
Date axes, blanks, stacked fills, markers, trendlines, and 3D perspective.
Exploded slices, donut holes, and projected leftovers.
Scatter variants, log axis pressure, and giant bubbles.
Polar charts and matrix-driven terrain plots.
High/low/close and a mismatched secondary-axis mashup.
Not a chart sheet: this is here to pressure-test styling and layout.
